statement: Instruction files for performance-critical C++ subsystems should explicitly address Data Oriented Design tradeoffs — for hot-path code with large data sets, structures of arrays and free functions routinely outperform class hierarchies in cache efficiency and inlinability.

evidenceRefs.title: r/cpp Software Architecture and Design thread (88 upvotes) — jmacey recommends DOD for high speed/throughput code alongside SOLID
r/cpp Design Patterns thread — TheTomato2 notes structs and free functions often beat OOP for data-heavy and simulation code
C++ Core Guidelines — Per.4: Do not assume complicated code is better than simple code

publicSummary: C++ instruction files for performance-critical subsystems should address DOD (structures of arrays, free functions, batch operations) alongside OOP patterns — practitioners consistently recommend data layout consideration for hot-path code.
